events.desc_en: Bessel measures the parallax of 61 Cygni, first confirmation of stellar distance by trigonometry.
events.amazon_title: A Short History of Nearly Everything — Bill Bryson

events.desc_zh: 觀測仙女座確認為獨立星系，宇宙不只銀河系
events.desc_en: Observing Andromeda confirms it as an independent galaxy; the universe extends far beyond the Milky Way.
events.amazon_title: The Day We Found the Universe — Marcia Bartusiak

events.desc_zh: 觀測星系紅移，證明宇宙正在膨脹
events.desc_en: Observing galactic redshifts, proving the universe is expanding.
events.amazon_title: Big Bang: The Origin of the Universe — Simon Singh

events.zh: 宇宙微波背景輻射發現
events.desc_zh: Penzias 與 Wilson 意外發現宇宙背景輻射，證實大爆炸理論
events.desc_en: Penzias and Wilson accidentally discover cosmic background radiation, confirming the Big Bang theory.
events.amazon_title: Wrinkles in Time — George Smoot and Keay Davidson

events.desc_zh: Jocelyn Bell 發現高速自轉中子星的規律無線電脈衝信號
events.desc_en: Jocelyn Bell discovers regular radio pulses from rapidly rotating neutron stars.
events.amazon_title: A Short History of Nearly Everything — Bill Bryson

events.desc_zh: 航海家 1 號/2 號探訪四顆外行星，航海家 1 號已進入星際空間
events.desc_en: Voyager 1 and 2 visit all four outer planets; Voyager 1 has entered interstellar space.
events.amazon_title: The Interstellar Age: The Story of the NASA Men and Women Who Flew the Forty-Year Voyager Mission — Jim Bell

events.desc_zh: 首個大型太空光學望遠鏡，拍攝深空影像改變人類宇宙觀
events.desc_en: First major space optical telescope; its deep field images transformed our view of the cosmos.
events.amazon_title: The Universe in a Mirror: The Saga of the Hubble Space Telescope and the Visionaries Who Built It — Robert Zimmerman

events.zh: 首顆太陽系外行星確認
events.desc_zh: 51 Pegasi b，首顆環繞類太陽恆星的系外行星
events.desc_en: 51 Pegasi b, the first exoplanet discovered orbiting a Sun-like star.
events.amazon_title: Strange New Worlds: The Search for Alien Planets and Life beyond Our Solar System — Ray Jayawardhana

events.desc_zh: 超新星觀測證實宇宙加速膨脹，暗能量佔宇宙 68%
events.desc_en: Supernova observations confirm accelerating expansion; dark energy comprises 68% of the universe.
events.amazon_title: The Extravagant Universe: Exploding Stars, Dark Energy, and the Accelerating Cosmos — Robert P. Kirshner

events.desc_zh: CERN 大型強子對撞機確認希格斯粒子，解釋粒子獲得質量的機制
events.desc_en: CERN's Large Hadron Collider confirms the Higgs boson, explaining how particles acquire mass.
events.amazon_title: CERN and the Higgs Boson: The Global Quest for the Building Blocks of Reality — James Gillies

events.desc_zh: LIGO 偵測雙黑洞合併產生的時空漣漪，驗證愛因斯坦百年前的預言
events.desc_en: LIGO detects spacetime ripples from merging black holes, confirming Einstein's century-old prediction.
events.amazon_title: Black Hole Blues and Other Songs from Outer Space — Janna Levin

events.desc_zh: 事件視界望遠鏡拍攝 M87 星系中心超大質量黑洞，史上首張黑洞照片
events.desc_en: Event Horizon Telescope captures the supermassive black hole at the center of M87, the first-ever black hole image.
events.amazon_title: Einstein's Shadow: The Inside Story of Astronomers' Decades-Long Quest to Take the First Picture of a Black Hole — Seth Fletcher

events.zh: JWST 韋伯太空望遠鏡啟用
events.desc_zh: 史上最強紅外線太空望遠鏡，觀測宇宙最早期星系與恆星
events.desc_en: The most powerful infrared space telescope ever, observing the earliest galaxies and stars in the universe.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/詹姆斯·韋伯太空望遠鏡
events.amazon_title: Unseen Universe: Space as you've never seen it before from the James Webb Space Telescope — Caroline Harper

events.zh: 第一代恆星點燃・宇宙黎明
events.desc_zh: 第一批恆星開始核融合發光，結束長達數億年的宇宙黑暗時代
events.desc_en: The first stars ignite nuclear fusion, ending the cosmic Dark Ages that lasted hundreds of millions of years.
events.amazon_title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry — Neil deGrasse Tyson

events.desc_zh: 恆星和類星體的紫外線將星際氫重新電離，宇宙再次變得透明
events.desc_en: UV light from stars and quasars reionizes intergalactic hydrogen; the universe becomes transparent again.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 宇宙約 20 億年時類星體數量達到巔峰，超大質量黑洞最活躍的時期
events.desc_en: Quasar numbers peak when the universe is ~2 billion years old; supermassive black holes are at their most active.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 物質主導時代・結構加速形成
events.desc_zh: 物質密度超過輻射，引力作用加速星系和星系團的形成
events.desc_en: Matter density exceeds radiation; gravity accelerates the formation of galaxies and galaxy clusters.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 星系中心黑洞周圍的燃料逐漸耗盡，類星體數量急劇下降
events.desc_en: Fuel around galactic central black holes depletes; quasar numbers drop sharply.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 宇宙恆星形成率降至高峰一半
events.en: Star Formation Drops to Half Peak
events.desc_zh: 宇宙可用氫氣持續消耗，恆星形成速率降至巔峰時期的一半
events.desc_en: Available hydrogen continues to deplete; the cosmic star formation rate drops to half its peak value.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 橢圓星系停止造星（紅與死）
events.desc_zh: 大量橢圓星系耗盡氣體停止造星，呈現紅色的老年恆星群
events.desc_en: Many elliptical galaxies exhaust their gas and stop forming stars, becoming 'red and dead'.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 宇宙膨脹速度持續加快
events.desc_zh: 暗能量持續主導，遠處星系退行速度越來越快
events.desc_en: Dark energy continues to dominate; distant galaxies recede at ever-increasing speeds.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 星系團之間的巨大空洞持續擴張，最大的牧夫座空洞直徑 3.3 億光年
events.desc_en: Vast voids between galaxy clusters keep expanding; the Boötes Void spans 330 million light-years.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 拉尼亞凱亞超星系團結構穩定
events.desc_zh: 包含銀河系在內的超星系團結構趨於穩定，跨越 5 億光年
events.desc_en: The supercluster containing the Milky Way stabilizes, spanning 500 million light-years.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: M4 是距地球最近的球狀星團之一，包含已知最古老的白矮星
events.desc_en: M4 is one of the nearest globular clusters to Earth, containing the oldest known white dwarfs.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 銀河系第二族星大量形成
events.en: Population II Stars Form En Masse
events.desc_zh: 含微量重元素的第二族星大量形成，構成銀暈和厚盤的主要恆星
events.desc_en: Population II stars with trace heavy elements form in large numbers, comprising the halo and thick disk.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 銀河系中心的棒狀核球區域穩定，包含大量古老恆星
events.desc_en: The bar-shaped bulge at the Milky Way's center stabilizes, containing predominantly old stars.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 銀河系恆星形成率下降
events.en: Milky Way Star Formation Rate Declines
events.desc_zh: 銀河系每年僅形成 1-2 顆新恆星，遠低於早期每年數十顆
events.desc_en: The Milky Way forms only 1-2 new stars per year, far below the dozens per year in its early history.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 銀河系薄盤旋臂結構成熟
events.desc_zh: 薄盤中的旋臂結構完全成熟，類似今日所見的壯觀旋臂
events.desc_en: Spiral arm structure in the thin disk fully matures into the grand design we see today.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 大小麥哲倫雲被銀河系捕獲
events.desc_zh: 大小麥哲倫雲成為銀河系衛星星系，麥哲倫星流形成
events.desc_en: The Large and Small Magellanic Clouds become satellite galaxies; the Magellanic Stream forms.
events.amazon_title: Galaxies: A Very Short Introduction — John Gribbin

events.desc_zh: 太陽所在的獵戶臂（本地臂）從英仙臂分支形成
events.desc_en: The Orion Arm (Local Arm) where our Sun resides branches off from the Perseus Arm.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 人馬座矮星系第二次穿越銀河盤
events.desc_zh: 人馬座矮星系再次穿越銀河盤面，觸發新一輪恆星形成
events.desc_en: Sagittarius Dwarf passes through the Milky Way disk again, triggering a new round of star formation.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 多次超新星爆炸在太陽周圍清出一個直徑 1000 光年的低密度氣泡
events.desc_en: Multiple supernovae clear a 1000-light-year low-density bubble around the Sun.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 太陽進入獵戶臂現在位置
events.en: Sun Enters Current Orion Arm Position
events.desc_zh: 太陽繞銀河系中心運行，抵達獵戶臂內側邊緣的現在位置
events.desc_en: The Sun, orbiting the galactic center, arrives at its current position on the inner edge of the Orion Arm.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/太陽系在銀河系中的位置
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 太陽系形成初期，大量小天體互相碰撞，行星表面佈滿撞擊坑
events.desc_en: In the early solar system, massive numbers of small bodies collide, cratering planetary surfaces.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 木星經過大遷移後穩定在距太陽 5.2 AU 的軌道
events.desc_en: After the Grand Tack migration, Jupiter settles into its current orbit at 5.2 AU from the Sun.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 失去磁場保護後，太陽風持續剝離火星大氣，火星變為寒冷荒漠
events.desc_en: Without magnetic field protection, solar wind strips Mars' atmosphere; Mars becomes a cold desert.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 泰坦、恩克拉多斯等主要衛星軌道穩定，泰坦發展出濃厚大氣
events.desc_en: Titan, Enceladus and other major moons stabilize; Titan develops a thick atmosphere.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.en: Jupiter's Great Red Spot May Form
events.desc_zh: 木星大氣中的巨大反氣旋風暴，持續數百年以上
events.desc_en: A giant anticyclonic storm in Jupiter's atmosphere, persisting for centuries or longer.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.desc_zh: 月球內部冷卻，最後的火山活動停止，月海不再有新的熔岩流
events.desc_en: The Moon's interior cools; final volcanic activity ceases, and no new lava flows fill the maria.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 太陽繞銀河系一圈（銀河年）
events.desc_zh: 太陽每 2.25 億年繞銀河系中心一圈，此時恰好是一個銀河年前
events.desc_en: The Sun takes 225 million years to orbit the galactic center; this marks exactly one galactic year ago.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 月球最後大型撞擊（第谷坑）
events.desc_zh: 直徑 85 公里的第谷坑形成，是月球上最年輕的大型撞擊坑之一
events.desc_en: 85km-wide Tycho crater forms, one of the youngest large impact craters on the Moon.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 歐特雲彗星週期性擾動
events.desc_zh: 經過恆星或銀河潮汐力週期性擾動歐特雲，將彗星送入內太陽系
events.desc_en: Passing stars or galactic tides periodically perturb the Oort Cloud, sending comets into the inner solar system.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 近地超新星觸發冰河期
events.en: Nearby Supernova May Trigger Ice Age
events.desc_zh: 距地球約 150 光年的超新星爆炸，可能影響地球氣候並觸發冰河期
events.desc_en: A supernova ~150 light-years from Earth may have affected climate and triggered glaciation.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 機會號・精神號登陸火星
events.desc_zh: NASA 雙胞胎火星探測車成功登陸，機會號運作超過 14 年
events.desc_en: NASA's twin Mars rovers land successfully; Opportunity operates for over 14 years.
events.amazon_title: Cosmos — Carl Sagan

events.zh: 毅力號登陸火星・機智號首飛
events.desc_zh: 毅力號成功登陸火星採集岩石樣本，機智號完成人類首次外星動力飛行
events.desc_en: Perseverance lands on Mars to collect rock samples; Ingenuity achieves the first powered flight on another planet.
events.amazon_title: The Mission: A True Story — David W. Brown

events.en: Yu the Great — Founder of Xia
events.desc_zh: 傳說中治水英雄，建立中國第一個世襲王朝。
events.desc_en: Legendary flood controller who founded China's first hereditary dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 商湯推翻夏桀，建立商朝。
events.desc_en: Tang overthrew the last Xia king and founded the Shang dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 嬴政統一六國，建立中國第一個中央集權帝國，統一度量衡、文字。
events.desc_en: Ying Zheng unified six states, founded the first centralized empire, standardized weights, measures, and writing.
events.amazon_title: The Early Chinese Empires: Qin and Han — Mark Edward Lewis

events.desc_zh: 開拓絲路、獨尊儒術、擊敗匈奴。漢朝全盛時期。
events.desc_en: Opened Silk Road, promoted Confucianism, defeated Xiongnu. Peak of Han dynasty.
events.amazon_title: The Early Chinese Empires: Qin and Han — Mark Edward Lewis

events.desc_zh: 曹操之子曹丕篡漢，三國鼎立開始。
events.desc_en: Cao Pi usurped the Han throne, beginning the Three Kingdoms period.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.en: Emperor Wen of Sui Reunifies China
events.desc_zh: 楊堅結束南北朝分裂，建立隋朝，推行均田制和科舉制。
events.desc_en: Yang Jian ended the North-South division, established the Sui dynasty and imperial examination system.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.zh: 唐太宗即位・貞觀之治
events.desc_zh: 李世民開創貞觀之治，唐朝進入全盛。
events.desc_en: Li Shimin ushered in the Zhenguan golden age of the Tang dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 中國歷史上唯一的女皇帝。改國號「周」。
events.desc_en: The only female emperor in Chinese history. Changed dynasty name to Zhou.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 陳橋兵變，黃袍加身，建立宋朝。
events.desc_en: Zhao Kuangyin seized power and founded the Song dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 鐵木真統一蒙古，建立史上最大連續領土帝國。
events.desc_en: Temüjin unified Mongol tribes and founded the largest contiguous land empire.
events.amazon_title: Genghis Khan and the Making of the Modern World — Jack Weatherford

events.desc_zh: 忽必烈定國號「元」，蒙古人統治中國。
events.desc_en: Kublai Khan proclaimed the Yuan dynasty, Mongol rule over China.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 農民起義推翻元朝，建立明朝。
events.desc_en: Peasant rebel overthrew Yuan and established the Ming dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 遷都北京、編纂永樂大典、派鄭和下西洋。
events.desc_en: Moved capital to Beijing, commissioned Yongle Encyclopedia, sent Zheng He's voyages.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 在位 61 年，平三藩、收台灣、抗俄羅斯。清朝盛世。
events.desc_en: Reigned 61 years. Suppressed revolts, annexed Taiwan, resisted Russia. Qing golden age.
events.amazon_title: The Search for Modern China — Jonathan D. Spence

events.desc_zh: 編纂四庫全書。乾隆盛世後清朝由盛轉衰。
events.desc_en: Compiled Siku Quanshu. After his reign, Qing dynasty began to decline.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.en: Puyi Abdicates — End of Imperial China
events.desc_zh: 末代皇帝溥儀退位，中國兩千年帝制結束。
events.desc_en: Last emperor Puyi abdicated, ending 2000 years of imperial rule in China.
events.amazon_title: The Search for Modern China — Jonathan D. Spence

events.desc_zh: 周武王伐紂，滅商建周。
events.desc_en: King Wu of Zhou defeated Shang at Muye, establishing the Zhou dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 秦趙之戰，秦將白起坑殺趙軍 40 萬。
events.desc_en: Qin general Bai Qi defeated Zhao and reportedly buried 400,000 soldiers alive.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 孫劉聯軍大敗曹操，三國鼎立格局確立。
events.desc_en: Sun-Liu alliance defeated Cao Cao, solidifying the Three Kingdoms division.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 東晉以少勝多擊敗前秦，南北對峙格局延續。
events.desc_en: Eastern Jin defeated Former Qin despite being outnumbered, preserving the North-South divide.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 安祿山叛亂，唐朝由盛轉衰的轉折點。
events.desc_en: An Lushan's rebellion marked the turning point of Tang dynasty from prosperity to decline.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 金兵攻陷汴京，北宋滅亡，南宋建立。
events.desc_en: Jin captured Kaifeng, ending Northern Song. Southern Song was established.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 英國以鴉片貿易為由開戰，清朝戰敗簽訂南京條約。
events.desc_en: Britain waged war over opium trade. Qing defeat led to Treaty of Nanjing.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 洪秀全領導的農民起義，造成約 2000 萬人死亡。
events.desc_en: Hong Xiuquan's peasant rebellion, one of the deadliest conflicts in history (~20M deaths).
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 英法聯軍攻入北京，火燒圓明園。
events.desc_en: Anglo-French forces entered Beijing and burned the Old Summer Palace.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 清朝敗給日本，割讓台灣，東亞格局劇變。
events.desc_en: Qing lost to Japan, ceded Taiwan. Dramatically shifted East Asian power dynamics.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 義和團運動引發八國聯軍入侵北京。
events.desc_en: Boxer Uprising led to an eight-nation military intervention in Beijing.
events.amazon_title: The Search for Modern China — Jonathan D. Spence

events.desc_zh: 武昌起義推翻清朝，建立中華民國。
events.desc_en: Wuchang Uprising overthrew the Qing dynasty, establishing the Republic of China.
events.amazon_title: The Search for Modern China — Jonathan D. Spence

events.desc_zh: 中國抗日戰爭，二戰亞洲戰場的一部分。
events.desc_en: China's war against Japan, part of WWII's Asian theater.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 國民黨與共產黨全面內戰。共產黨勝利，國民黨退守台灣。
events.desc_en: Full-scale civil war between KMT and CPC. CPC victory; KMT retreated to Taiwan.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 中國最早的詩歌總集，收錄西周至春秋時期 305 首詩。
events.desc_en: China's earliest poetry anthology, containing 305 poems from the Western Zhou to Spring and Autumn periods.
events.amazon_title: The Book of Songs: The Ancient Chinese Classic of Poetry — Arthur Waley

events.desc_zh: 屈原創作《離騷》等浪漫主義詩篇。
events.desc_en: Qu Yuan wrote Li Sao and other romantic poems, founding the Chu Ci literary tradition.
events.amazon_title: The Songs of the South: An Anthology of Ancient Chinese Poems — David Hawkes

events.en: Sima Qian — Records of the Grand Historian
events.desc_zh: 中國第一部紀傳體通史，被譽為「史家之絕唱」。
events.desc_en: China's first comprehensive biographical history, praised as the supreme work of historiography.
events.amazon_title: Records of the Grand Historian: Han Dynasty — Sima Qian, Burton Watson (translator)

events.desc_zh: 描繪世外桃源，開創田園文學。
events.desc_en: Depicted an idyllic utopia, pioneering pastoral literature.
events.amazon_title: The Selected Poems of T'ao Ch'ien — David Hinton

events.desc_zh: 李白、杜甫、白居易等詩人輩出，唐詩達到巔峰。
events.desc_en: Li Bai, Du Fu, Bai Juyi and other great poets flourished. Peak of Chinese poetry.
events.amazon_title: The Jade Mountain: Three Hundred Tang Poems — Witter Bynner

events.desc_zh: 蘇軾、李清照、辛棄疾等人將詞推向頂峰。
events.desc_en: Su Shi, Li Qingzhao, Xin Qiji elevated Ci poetry to its zenith.
events.amazon_title: Among the Flowers: The Hua-chien chi — Lois Fusek

events.desc_zh: 關漢卿《竇娥冤》、王實甫《西廂記》，中國戲劇文學興起。
events.desc_en: Guan Hanqing and Wang Shifu pioneered Chinese dramatic literature.
events.amazon_title: Selected Plays of the Yuan Dynasty — Richard F.S. Yang

events.desc_zh: 羅貫中著，中國四大名著之一。
events.desc_en: By Luo Guanzhong. One of the Four Great Classical Novels of Chinese literature.
events.amazon_title: Three Kingdoms: A Historical Novel — Luo Guanzhong, Moss Roberts (translator)

events.desc_zh: 吳承恩著，講述唐三藏西天取經。四大名著之一。
events.desc_en: By Wu Cheng'en. The story of Xuanzang's pilgrimage. One of the Four Great Classical Novels.
events.amazon_title: Journey to the West — Wu Cheng'en, Anthony C. Yu (translator)

events.desc_zh: 施耐庵著，108 好漢的故事。四大名著之一。
events.desc_en: By Shi Nai'an. Story of 108 outlaws. One of the Four Great Classical Novels.
events.amazon_title: Outlaws of the Marsh — Shi Nai'an, Sidney Shapiro (translator)

events.desc_zh: 曹雪芹著，中國文學最高成就之一。四大名著之一。
events.desc_en: By Cao Xueqin. Widely regarded as the pinnacle of Chinese fiction. One of the Four Great Classical Novels.
events.amazon_title: The Story of the Stone: The Golden Days — Cao Xueqin, David Hawkes (translator)

events.desc_zh: 中國現代文學開山之作。
events.desc_en: The founding work of modern Chinese literature.
events.amazon_title: The Real Story of Ah-Q and Other Tales of China — Lu Xun, Julia Lovell (translator)

events.desc_zh: 劉秀復漢，定都洛陽。
events.desc_en: Liu Xiu restored the Han dynasty with the capital at Luoyang.
events.amazon_title: The Early Chinese Empires: Qin and Han — Mark Edward Lewis

events.desc_zh: 中國共產黨建立的社會主義國家。
events.desc_en: Socialist state established by the Chinese Communist Party.
events.amazon_title: The Tragedy of Liberation: A History of the Chinese Revolution 1945-1957 — Frank Dikötter

events.desc_zh: 日本投降，台灣結束 50 年日治，回歸中華民國。
events.desc_en: Japan surrendered. Taiwan returned to the Republic of China after 50 years of Japanese rule.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 民主運動人士在高雄遊行遭鎮壓，台灣民主化的重要轉折。
events.desc_en: Pro-democracy rally in Kaohsiung was suppressed, becoming a turning point for Taiwan's democratization.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.zh: 蔣經國去世・李登輝繼任
events.en: Chiang Ching-kuo Dies, Lee Teng-hui Succeeds
events.desc_zh: 蔣經國去世，李登輝成為首位台灣出生的總統，開啟民主轉型。
events.desc_en: Chiang Ching-kuo died. Lee Teng-hui became the first Taiwan-born president, initiating democratic reforms.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 陳水扁當選總統，國民黨首次失去政權。
events.desc_en: Chen Shui-bian elected president. KMT lost power for the first time.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 反服貿學生運動佔領立法院，影響台灣政治走向。
events.desc_en: Anti-CSSTA student movement occupied the Legislative Yuan, reshaping Taiwan's political landscape.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 蔡英文當選總統，民進黨重新執政。
events.desc_en: Tsai Ing-wen elected president. DPP returned to power.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 蔣經國推動十大建設，台灣經濟起飛。
events.desc_en: Chiang Ching-kuo launched Ten Major Construction Projects, driving Taiwan's economic takeoff.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 台灣半導體產業基地，孕育台積電、聯電等。
events.desc_en: Foundation of Taiwan's semiconductor industry base, incubating TSMC, UMC, etc.
events.amazon_title: Chip War: The Fight for the World's Most Critical Technology — Chris Miller

events.desc_zh: 台北至高雄 345 公里，最快 90 分鐘，南北一日生活圈。
events.desc_en: 345 km from Taipei to Kaohsiung in 90 minutes. Created a one-day commuting circle.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 台積電市值超越三星，成為全球最大半導體代工廠。
events.desc_en: TSMC surpassed Samsung to become the world's largest semiconductor foundry.
events.amazon_title: Chip War: The Fight for the World's Most Critical Technology — Chris Miller

events.desc_zh: 荷蘭傳教士以羅馬拼音記錄原住民語言，留下新港文書。
events.desc_en: Dutch missionaries recorded indigenous languages in Roman script, creating the Sinckan Manuscripts.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 閩粵移民大量湧入台灣，漢人逐漸成為主要人口。
events.desc_en: Massive immigration from Fujian and Guangdong. Han Chinese gradually became the dominant population.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 日本藉琉球漂民事件出兵台灣，清朝開始重視台灣防務。
events.desc_en: Japan sent troops to Taiwan over the Mudan incident. Qing began to prioritize Taiwan's defense.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 基隆至高雄縱貫鐵路全線通車，日治重大基礎建設。
events.desc_en: Keelung-Kaohsiung trunk railway completed. Major Japanese-era infrastructure.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 林獻堂等人成立，推動台灣人自覺運動。
events.desc_en: Founded by Lin Xiantang and others to promote Taiwanese cultural awareness.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 台灣大學前身，日治時期最高學府。
events.desc_en: Predecessor of National Taiwan University. The highest institution of learning during Japanese rule.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 八田與一設計，東亞最大水利工程，嘉南平原成為穀倉。
events.desc_en: Designed by Hatta Yoichi. Largest irrigation system in East Asia. Transformed Jianan Plain into a granary.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 日本推行同化政策，要求台灣人改日本姓名、參拜神社。
events.desc_en: Japan enforced assimilation: Japanese names, Shinto worship, and Japanese-language-only policies.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 戒嚴下的政治迫害，數萬人受害。
events.desc_en: Political persecution under martial law. Tens of thousands affected.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 語言學證據指台灣為南島語族發源地，向南擴散至太平洋。
events.desc_en: Linguistic evidence suggests Taiwan as the origin point of Austronesian dispersal across the Pacific.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 東亞人類學之父，四次來台系統調查原住民文化。
events.desc_en: Father of East Asian anthropology. Conducted four systematic surveys of Taiwan's indigenous peoples.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 提出台灣原住民「4群8族11部」分類體系。
events.desc_en: Proposed the '4 groups, 8 tribes, 11 divisions' classification of Taiwan's indigenous peoples.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 「生蕃行腳」作者。因無力阻止五年理蕃計畫而投海自盡。
events.desc_en: Author of 'Journeys Among the Savages'. Committed suicide at sea, unable to stop the aboriginal subjugation policy.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 阿美族七腳川社抗日事件，日軍焚毀部落並強制遷移。
events.desc_en: Amis people of Chikaokawan resisted Japanese rule. The village was burned and residents forcibly relocated.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 佐久間左馬太總督武力征服原住民部落，沒收槍械。
events.desc_en: Governor Sakuma's military campaign to subjugate indigenous tribes and confiscate weapons.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 「八二粁一四五米」作者。博物學家，深入台灣高山進行生態調查，1945年失蹤於北婆羅洲。
events.desc_en: Author of '82km 145m'. Naturalist who explored Taiwan's mountains. Disappeared in North Borneo in 1945.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 「山胞」正式改稱「原住民」，消弭族稱歧視。
events.desc_en: 'Mountain compatriots' officially renamed to 'indigenous peoples', ending discriminatory terminology.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/臺灣原住民族正名運動
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.zh: 平埔原住民族群身分法
events.desc_zh: 立法院通過，西拉雅等九族可申請平埔原住民身分。
events.desc_en: Legislature passed law allowing Siraya and eight other plains indigenous groups to apply for official recognition.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.en: Hou Hsiao-hsien — A City of Sadness
events.desc_zh: 威尼斯金獅獎，首部觸及二二八的台灣電影。
events.desc_en: Venice Golden Lion. First Taiwanese film to address the 228 Incident.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 台灣導演李安獲奧斯卡最佳外語片，台灣電影國際突破。
events.desc_en: Taiwanese director Ang Lee won Oscar for Best Foreign Language Film. International breakthrough for Taiwan cinema.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 台灣文化輸出代表，歌聲傳遍華人世界與日本。
events.desc_en: Icon of Taiwanese cultural export. Her voice reached the entire Chinese-speaking world and Japan.
events.amazon_title: A New Illustrated History of Taiwan — Wan-yao Chou

events.desc_zh: 安祿山叛亂，唐朝由盛轉衰的轉折點。
events.desc_en: An Lushan's rebellion marked the turning point of Tang dynasty from prosperity to decline.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 英國以鴉片貿易為由開戰，清朝戰敗簽訂南京條約。
events.desc_en: Britain waged war over opium trade. Qing defeat led to Treaty of Nanjing.
events.amazon_title: The Opium War: Drugs, Dreams, and the Making of Modern China — Julia Lovell

events.desc_zh: 洪秀全領導的農民起義，造成約 2000 萬人死亡。
events.desc_en: Hong Xiuquan's peasant rebellion, one of the deadliest conflicts in history (~20M deaths).
events.amazon_title: Autumn in the Heavenly Kingdom: China, the West, and the Epic Story of the Taiping Civil War — Stephen R. Platt

events.desc_zh: 清朝敗給日本，割讓台灣，東亞格局劇變。
events.desc_en: Qing lost to Japan, ceded Taiwan. Dramatically shifted East Asian power dynamics.
events.amazon_title: The Search for Modern China — Jonathan D. Spence

events.desc_zh: 中國抗日戰爭，二戰亞洲戰場的一部分。
events.desc_en: China's war against Japan, part of WWII's Asian theater.
events.amazon_title: Forgotten Ally: China's World War II, 1937-1945 — Rana Mitter

events.desc_zh: 國民黨與共產黨全面內戰。共產黨勝利，國民黨退守台灣。
events.desc_en: Full-scale civil war between KMT and CPC. CPC victory; KMT retreated to Taiwan.
events.amazon_title: The Tragedy of Liberation: A History of the Chinese Revolution 1945-1957 — Frank Dikötter

events.desc_zh: 李白、杜甫、白居易等詩人輩出，唐詩達到巔峰。
events.desc_en: Li Bai, Du Fu, Bai Juyi and other great poets flourished. Peak of Chinese poetry.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 周武王姬發在牧野之戰滅商，建立周朝。
events.desc_en: King Wu defeated Shang at Muye and established the Zhou dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 秦始皇死後，胡亥即位，暴政導致農民起義。
events.desc_en: After Qin Shi Huang's death, Hu Hai's tyranny led to peasant uprisings.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 平民出身，推翻秦朝，建立漢朝。
events.desc_en: A commoner who overthrew the Qin and founded the Han dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.en: Wang Mang Usurps Han — Xin Dynasty
events.desc_zh: 王莽篡奪西漢政權，建立短命的新朝（9-23年）。
events.desc_en: Wang Mang usurped the Western Han throne and established the short-lived Xin dynasty (9-23 CE).
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 唐太宗李世民，唐朝第二任皇帝。
events.desc_en: Li Shimin, the second emperor of Tang, born.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 朱元璋廢除丞相制度，皇權空前集中。
events.desc_en: Hongwu Emperor abolished the chancellor system, centralizing imperial power.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 項羽與劉邦爭天下，劉邦最終建立漢朝。
events.desc_en: Xiang Yu and Liu Bang fought for supremacy. Liu Bang won and founded the Han dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 張角領導的農民起義，東漢末年動亂之始。
events.desc_en: Peasant revolt led by Zhang Jue, beginning the end of the Eastern Han.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.en: Battle of Yamen — Fall of Song
events.desc_zh: 蒙元滅南宋最後一戰，陸秀夫背幼帝投海。
events.desc_en: Final battle between Yuan and Song. Lu Xiufu carried the child emperor into the sea.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 農民軍攻入北京，崇禎帝自縊，明朝滅亡。
events.desc_en: Rebel army entered Beijing. Chongzhen Emperor hanged himself. End of Ming dynasty.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 法國爭奪越南宗主權，與清朝開戰。
events.desc_en: France fought Qing China over suzerainty of Vietnam.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.desc_zh: 孔子弟子編纂，儒家核心經典。
events.desc_en: Compiled by Confucius' disciples. The core text of Confucianism.
events.amazon_title: The Analects — Confucius, D.C. Lau (translator)

events.en: Sun Tzu — The Art of War
events.desc_zh: 世界最早的軍事著作，影響東西方軍事思想。
events.desc_en: The world's earliest military treatise, influencing military thought globally.
events.amazon_title: The Art of War — Sun Tzu, Samuel B. Griffith (translator)

events.desc_zh: 道家哲學核心經典，影響中國思想兩千年。
events.desc_en: Core text of Daoist philosophy, influencing Chinese thought for two millennia.
events.amazon_title: Tao Te Ching — Lao Tzu, Stephen Mitchell (translator)

events.desc_zh: 中國第一部編年體通史，記載戰國至五代的歷史。
events.desc_en: China's first comprehensive chronological history, covering Warring States to Five Dynasties.
events.amazon_title: China: A History — John Keay

events.en: The Plum in the Golden Vase
events.desc_zh: 中國第一部文人獨立創作的長篇小說。
events.desc_en: The first full-length novel written independently by a single Chinese author.
events.amazon_title: The Plum in the Golden Vase or, Chin P'ing Mei — David Tod Roy

events.desc_zh: 商朝刻在龜甲獸骨上的文字，中國最早的成熟文字系統。
events.desc_en: Earliest known mature Chinese writing, carved on oracle bones during the Shang dynasty.
events.amazon_title: The Genius of China: 3,000 Years of Science, Discovery, and Invention — Robert Temple

events.desc_zh: 中國發明的計算工具，使用至今。
events.desc_en: Chinese-invented calculation tool, still used today.
events.amazon_title: The Genius of China: 3,000 Years of Science, Discovery, and Invention — Robert Temple

events.desc_zh: 中國瓷器技術成熟，「China」之名即源於此。
events.desc_en: Chinese porcelain technology matured. The word 'China' derives from this craft.
events.amazon_title: The Genius of China: 3,000 Years of Science, Discovery, and Invention — Robert Temple

events.desc_zh: 世界現存最早的有日期標記的印刷書籍。
events.desc_en: The world's oldest dated printed book.
events.amazon_title: The Genius of China: 3,000 Years of Science, Discovery, and Invention — Robert Temple

events.desc_zh: 鄭和寶船長約 120 米，當時世界最大的木造船。
events.desc_en: Zheng He's treasure ships were ~120m long, the largest wooden ships of their time.
events.amazon_title: When China Ruled the Seas: The Treasure Fleet of the Dragon Throne, 1405-1433 — Louise Levathes

events.desc_zh: 最早的恐龍出現在三疊紀晚期的南美洲（現阿根廷）。
events.desc_en: Earliest dinosaurs appeared in Late Triassic South America (modern Argentina).
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Dinosaurs: A New History of a Lost World — Steve Brusatte

events.desc_zh: 包括獸腳類（暴龍、伶盜龍）和蜥腳類（腕龍、梁龍）。鳥類由此演化。
events.desc_en: Includes theropods (T. Rex, Velociraptor) and sauropods (Brachiosaurus, Diplodocus). Birds evolved from this group.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: How They Lived and Evolved — Darren Naish and Paul Barrett

events.desc_zh: 包括劍龍、三角龍、甲龍、副龍櫛龍等。全部為草食性。
events.desc_en: Includes Stegosaurus, Triceratops, Ankylosaurus, Parasaurolophus. All herbivorous.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Most Complete, Up-to-Date Encyclopedia for Dinosaur Lovers of All Ages — Thomas R. Holtz Jr.

events.desc_zh: 三疊紀末大滅絕消滅了大部分競爭者，恐龍成為陸地霸主。
events.desc_en: End-Triassic extinction eliminated most competitors. Dinosaurs became dominant land animals.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/三疊紀－侏羅紀滅絕事件
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs Rediscovered: The Scientific Revolution in Paleontology — Michael J. Benton

events.desc_zh: 蜥腳類恐龍體型達到巔峰，梁龍、腕龍等巨型草食恐龍遍布各大洲。
events.desc_en: Sauropods reached peak body size. Giant herbivores like Diplodocus and Brachiosaurus spread across continents.
events.amazon_title: The Sauropod Dinosaurs: Life in the Age of Giants — Mark Hallett and Mathew J. Wedel

events.desc_zh: 始祖鳥等過渡物種顯示鳥類由獸腳類恐龍演化而來。
events.desc_en: Transitional species like Archaeopteryx show birds evolved from theropod dinosaurs.
events.amazon_title: Feathered Dinosaurs: The Origin of Birds — John Long and Peter Schouten

events.zh: 被子植物改變恐龍生態
events.desc_zh: 開花植物大爆發改變草食恐龍的食物來源和生態系統。
events.desc_en: Flowering plant explosion changed food sources and ecosystems for herbivorous dinosaurs.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaur Odyssey: Fossil Threads in the Web of Life — Scott D. Sampson

events.desc_zh: 白堊紀晚期恐龍種類最多，暴龍、三角龍、甲龍等同時存在。
events.desc_en: Late Cretaceous saw maximum dinosaur diversity. T. Rex, Triceratops, Ankylosaurus coexisted.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Grand Tour, Second Edition — Keiron Pim, Fabio Pastori, and Jack Horner

events.desc_zh: 小行星撞擊尤卡坦半島，非鳥恐龍全部滅絕。只有鳥類存活至今。
events.desc_en: Asteroid struck Yucatán. All non-avian dinosaurs went extinct. Only birds survived to present day.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/白堊紀－古近紀滅絕事件
events.amazon_title: The Last Days of the Dinosaurs: An Asteroid, Extinction, and the Beginning of Our World — Riley Black

events.desc_zh: 最早的恐龍之一，體長約 1 米，雜食性。發現於阿根廷。
events.desc_en: One of the earliest dinosaurs. ~1m long, omnivorous. Discovered in Argentina.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Dinosaurs: A New History of a Lost World — Steve Brusatte

events.desc_zh: 三疊紀早期掠食者，體長約 6 米。
events.desc_en: Early Triassic predator, ~6m long.
events.amazon_title: The Complete Dinosaur, Second Edition — Michael K. Brett-Surman, Thomas R. Holtz, and James O. Farlow

events.desc_zh: 早期大型草食恐龍，體長可達 10 米。歐洲常見。
events.desc_en: Early large herbivore, up to 10m. Common in Europe.
events.amazon_title: My Beloved Brontosaurus: On the Road with Old Bones, New Science, and Our Favorite Dinosaurs — Brian Switek

events.desc_zh: 三疊紀代表性小型肉食恐龍，群居行為化石豐富。
events.desc_en: Iconic small Triassic predator. Rich fossil evidence of pack behavior.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs (DK Children's Visual Encyclopedias) — DK

events.desc_zh: 頭頂雙冠的早期侏羅紀肉食恐龍。
events.desc_en: Early Jurassic predator with distinctive twin crests on its head.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Grand Tour, Second Edition — Keiron Pim, Fabio Pastori, and Jack Horner

events.desc_zh: 在南極洲發現的肉食恐龍，證明侏羅紀南極氣候溫暖。
events.desc_en: Carnivore found in Antarctica, proving warm Jurassic polar climate.
events.amazon_title: The Princeton Field Guide to Dinosaurs, Third Edition — Gregory S. Paul

events.desc_zh: 發現於中國四川的蜥腳類恐龍，尾部有骨錘。
events.desc_en: Sauropod discovered in Sichuan, China. Had a bony tail club.
events.amazon_title: The Sauropod Dinosaurs: Life in the Age of Giants — Mark Hallett and Mathew J. Wedel

events.desc_zh: 侏羅紀頂級掠食者，體長可達 12 米。
events.desc_en: Apex Jurassic predator, up to 12m long.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: How They Lived and Evolved — Darren Naish and Paul Barrett

events.desc_zh: 背部有骨板、尾部有尖刺的草食恐龍。腦容量極小。
events.desc_en: Herbivore with back plates and tail spikes. Famously tiny brain.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Most Complete, Up-to-Date Encyclopedia for Dinosaur Lovers of All Ages — Thomas R. Holtz Jr.

events.desc_zh: 巨型蜥腳類，前腿比後腿長，頸部可達 9 米高。
events.desc_en: Giant sauropod with longer forelimbs. Neck could reach 9m high.
events.amazon_title: The Sauropod Dinosaurs: Life in the Age of Giants — Mark Hallett and Mathew J. Wedel

events.desc_zh: 體長可達 27 米的長頸恐龍，以尾巴甩擊防禦。
events.desc_en: Up to 27m long. Used its whip-like tail for defense.
events.amazon_title: My Beloved Brontosaurus: On the Road with Old Bones, New Science, and Our Favorite Dinosaurs — Brian Switek

events.desc_zh: 翼龍不是恐龍，但同為中生代空中霸主。翼展最大可達 12 米。
events.desc_en: Pterosaurs were not dinosaurs but dominated Mesozoic skies. Wingspan up to 12m.
events.amazon_title: Pterosaurs: Natural History, Evolution, Anatomy — Mark P. Witton

events.desc_zh: 已知最大的肉食恐龍，體長可達 18 米。背上有巨大帆狀突起，可能半水生。
events.desc_en: Largest known carnivorous dinosaur, up to 18m. Had a large sail and was possibly semi-aquatic.
events.amazon_title: The Princeton Field Guide to Dinosaurs, Third Edition — Gregory S. Paul

events.desc_zh: 已知最大的恐龍之一，體重估計 70-80 噸。
events.desc_en: One of the largest known dinosaurs. Estimated weight 70-80 tonnes.
events.amazon_title: The Sauropod Dinosaurs: Life in the Age of Giants — Mark Hallett and Mathew J. Wedel

events.desc_zh: 頭頂有管狀冠飾的鴨嘴龍，可能用來發出低頻聲音。
events.desc_en: Hadrosaur with tubular crest, possibly used for producing low-frequency sounds.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Grand Tour, Second Edition — Keiron Pim, Fabio Pastori, and Jack Horner

events.desc_zh: 小型聰明的肉食恐龍，實際體型只有火雞大小，有羽毛。
events.desc_en: Small intelligent predator. Actually turkey-sized with feathers, unlike movie depictions.
events.amazon_title: Raptor Red: A Novel — Dr. Robert T. Bakker

events.desc_zh: 外形像鴕鳥的恐龍，跑速可達 60 km/h。
events.desc_en: Ostrich-like dinosaur. Could run up to 60 km/h.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs Rediscovered: The Scientific Revolution in Paleontology — Michael J. Benton

events.desc_zh: 擁有 70 公分長巨爪的神秘恐龍，草食性。
events.desc_en: Mysterious herbivore with 70cm-long claws.
events.amazon_title: The Princeton Field Guide to Dinosaurs, Third Edition — Gregory S. Paul

events.desc_zh: 白堊紀海洋頂級掠食者，體長可達 17 米。不是恐龍，是海洋爬蟲類。
events.desc_en: Top marine predator of the Cretaceous, up to 17m. Not a dinosaur but a marine reptile.
events.amazon_title: Ancient Sea Reptiles: Plesiosaurs, Ichthyosaurs, Mosasaurs, and More — Darren Naish

events.desc_zh: 頭頂有厚達 25 公分的骨質圓頂，可能用於頭部撞擊。
events.desc_en: Had a 25cm-thick dome of bone on its skull, possibly used for head-butting.
events.amazon_title: Dinosaurs: The Most Complete, Up-to-Date Encyclopedia for Dinosaur Lovers of All Ages — Thomas R. Holtz Jr.

events.desc_zh: 全身覆蓋骨甲的草食恐龍，尾部有骨錘可擊碎暴龍腿骨。
events.desc_en: Armored herbivore. Tail club could shatter a T. Rex's leg bones.
events.amazon_title: The Princeton Field Guide to Dinosaurs, Third Edition — Gregory S. Paul

events.desc_zh: 三根角 + 頸盾的草食恐龍，暴龍的主要獵物和勁敵。
events.desc_en: Three-horned herbivore with neck frill. Primary prey and rival of T. Rex.
events.amazon_title: The Horned Dinosaurs: A Natural History — Peter Dodson

events.desc_zh: 白堊紀末頂級掠食者，咬合力超過 5 萬牛頓。恐龍中最知名的物種。
events.desc_en: Ultimate Cretaceous apex predator. Bite force over 50,000 N. The most famous dinosaur species.
events.amazon_title: The Tyrannosaur Chronicles: The Biology of the Tyrant Dinosaurs — David Hone

events.desc_zh: 已知最大的飛行動物，翼展可達 12 米。站立時高如長頸鹿。
events.desc_en: Largest known flying animal. Wingspan up to 12m. Stood as tall as a giraffe.
events.amazon_title: Pterosaurs: Natural History, Evolution, Anatomy — Mark P. Witton

events.desc_zh: 恐龍滅絕後的復甦期。哺乳類開始填補生態空缺，體型逐漸增大。
events.desc_en: Recovery period after dinosaur extinction. Mammals began filling ecological niches and growing larger.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Reign of the Mammals: A New History, from the Shadow of the Dinosaurs to Us — Steve Brusatte

events.desc_zh: 新生代最溫暖的時期。現代哺乳類的主要類群出現：馬、鯨魚、蝙蝠、靈長類。
events.desc_en: Warmest period of the Cenozoic. Major modern mammal groups appeared: horses, whales, bats, primates.
events.amazon_title: After the Dinosaurs: The Age of Mammals — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 全球降溫，森林退縮，草原擴張。哺乳類體型增大，現代類群成形。
events.desc_en: Global cooling. Forests retreated, grasslands expanded. Mammals grew larger, modern groups took shape.
events.amazon_title: After the Dinosaurs: The Age of Mammals — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 草原時代。哺乳類多樣性巔峰。猿類多樣化，巨齒鯊稱霸海洋。
events.desc_en: Age of grasslands. Peak mammal diversity. Apes diversified. Megalodon ruled the seas.
events.amazon_title: The Real Planet of the Apes: A New Story of Human Origins — David R. Begun

events.desc_zh: 持續降溫。南北美洲相連（巴拿馬地峽）。人猿分化，直立行走的猿人出現。
events.desc_en: Continued cooling. Americas connected via Panama Isthmus. Human-ape split. Bipedal apes appeared.
events.amazon_title: First Steps: How Upright Walking Made Us Human — Jeremy DeSilva

events.desc_zh: 冰河時代。長毛象、劍齒虎等巨型動物。人屬出現並擴散全球。
events.desc_en: Ice Age. Megafauna like mammoths and saber-toothed cats. Genus Homo appeared and spread globally.
events.amazon_title: Frozen Earth: The Once and Future Story of Ice Ages — Doug Macdougall

events.desc_zh: 冰河期結束，人類文明興起。農業革命、城市、工業革命。
events.desc_en: End of Ice Age. Rise of human civilization. Agriculture, cities, Industrial Revolution.
events.amazon_title: The Long Summer: How Climate Changed Civilization — Brian Fagan

events.desc_zh: 恐龍滅絕後百萬年內，哺乳類快速多樣化，填補所有生態空缺。
events.desc_en: Within a million years of dinosaur extinction, mammals rapidly diversified to fill all ecological niches.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Reign of the Mammals: A New History, from the Shadow of the Dinosaurs to Us — Steve Brusatte

events.desc_zh: 巨型肉食鳥類（駭鳥）成為南美洲頂級掠食者，有些身高超過 2 米。
events.desc_en: Giant predatory birds (Phorusrhacids) became apex predators in South America. Some over 2m tall.
events.amazon_title: After the Dinosaurs: The Age of Mammals — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 囓齒類出現，日後成為哺乳類中物種數量最多的類群。
events.desc_en: Rodents appeared, eventually becoming the most species-rich order of mammals.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 原始食肉目（小古貓等）出現，是現代貓、狗、熊的祖先。
events.desc_en: Early carnivorans (Miacids) appeared — ancestors of modern cats, dogs, and bears.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 古新世-始新世極熱事件。全球溫度暴升 5-8°C，海洋酸化，大量物種遷徙。
events.desc_en: Global temperature surged 5-8°C. Ocean acidification. Massive species migration.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/古新世-始新世極熱事件
events.amazon_title: Beasts Before Us: The Untold Story of Mammal Origins and Evolution — Elsa Panciroli

events.desc_zh: 最早的馬，只有狗的大小，有四趾。五千萬年間逐漸演化為現代馬。
events.desc_en: Earliest horse, dog-sized with four toes. Evolved into modern horses over 50 million years.
events.amazon_title: After the Dinosaurs: The Age of Mammals — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 巴基鯨（Pakicetus）是有腳的鯨魚祖先。從陸地哺乳類演化為海洋巨獸。
events.desc_en: Pakicetus — a whale ancestor with legs. Mammals evolved from land back into the ocean.
events.amazon_title: Spying on Whales: The Past, Present, and Future of Earth's Most Awesome Creatures — Nick Pyenson

events.desc_zh: 最早的飛行哺乳類出現，回聲定位能力讓蝙蝠成功佔據夜間空中生態位。
events.desc_en: First flying mammals appeared. Echolocation enabled bats to dominate nocturnal aerial niches.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 體長可達 18 米的早期鯨魚，已完全適應海洋生活但仍保留退化後肢。
events.desc_en: Up to 18m long. Fully aquatic but still had vestigial hind limbs.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 全球急劇降溫（Grande Coupure），歐洲大量哺乳類滅絕，被亞洲物種取代。
events.desc_en: Dramatic global cooling (Grande Coupure). Many European mammals went extinct, replaced by Asian species.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/始新世-漸新世滅絕事件
events.amazon_title: The Ends of the World: Volcanic Apocalypses, Lethal Oceans, and Our Quest to Understand Earth's Past Mass Extinctions — Peter Brannen

events.desc_zh: 史上最大陸地哺乳類，肩高約 5 米，體重約 15-20 噸。比大象大一倍。
events.desc_en: Largest land mammal ever. ~5m at shoulder, ~15-20 tonnes. Twice the size of an elephant.
events.amazon_title: After the Dinosaurs: The Age of Mammals — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 全球降溫，森林退縮，草原擴張。改變哺乳類演化方向：跑得快的活下來。
events.desc_en: Global cooling caused forests to retreat and grasslands to expand. Survival favored speed.
events.amazon_title: Origins: How Earth's History Shaped Human History — Lewis Dartnell

events.desc_zh: 象類祖先開始體型增大，發展出長鼻和象牙。
events.desc_en: Elephant ancestors grew larger, developing trunks and tusks.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 猿類與舊世界猴分歧。猿類沒有尾巴、腦容量較大。
events.desc_en: Apes diverged from Old World monkeys. Apes lacked tails and had larger brains.
events.amazon_title: The Real Planet of the Apes: A New Story of Human Origins — David R. Begun

events.desc_zh: 史上最大鯊魚，體長可達 18 米，咬合力是暴龍的 6 倍。統治海洋 1300 萬年。
events.desc_en: Largest shark ever, up to 18m. Bite force 6× that of T. Rex. Ruled the oceans for 13 million years.
events.amazon_title: The Story of Life in 25 Fossils: Tales of Intrepid Fossil Hunters and the Wonders of Evolution — Donald R. Prothero

events.desc_zh: 各種古猿遍布非洲、歐洲和亞洲。原康修爾猿等為人類遠祖。
events.desc_en: Diverse ape species spread across Africa, Europe, and Asia. Proconsul among early human ancestors.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 馬從四趾演化為單蹄、體型增大、腿變長，適應草原奔跑。
events.desc_en: Horses evolved from four toes to single hoof, grew larger with longer legs for running on grasslands.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 劍齒虎科出現，犬齒長達 28 公分，是頂級掠食者。存活至約 1 萬年前。
events.desc_en: Saber-toothed cats appeared with 28cm canines. Apex predators surviving until ~10,000 years ago.
events.amazon_title: End of the Megafauna: The Fate of the World's Hugest, Fiercest, and Strangest Animals — Ross D.E. MacPhee

events.desc_zh: 猿類從非洲擴散到歐洲和亞洲，為日後人猿分化鋪路。
events.desc_en: Apes migrated from Africa to Europe and Asia, setting stage for the human-ape divergence.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.zh: 巴拿馬地峽形成・南北美大交換
events.desc_zh: 南北美洲相連，動物大遷徙。北美的貓科、犬科南下；南美的犰狳、負鼠北上。
events.desc_en: Americas connected. Cats and dogs went south; armadillos and opossums went north.
events.amazon_title: Cowen's History of Life — Michael J. Benton

events.desc_zh: 海洋降溫和鯨魚競爭導致巨齒鯊滅絕。
events.desc_en: Ocean cooling and whale competition drove Megalodon to extinction.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 適應冰河期的巨型象，遍布北半球。約 4000 年前最後的族群在西伯利亞滅絕。
events.desc_en: Ice Age giant spread across Northern Hemisphere. Last population went extinct in Siberia ~4,000 years ago.
events.amazon_title: Woolly: The True Story of the Quest to Revive One of History's Most Iconic Extinct Creatures — Ben Mezrich

events.desc_zh: 體重可達 4 噸的巨型地面樹懶，站立時高 6 米。約 1 萬年前滅絕。
events.desc_en: 4-tonne giant ground sloth, 6m tall when standing. Extinct ~10,000 years ago.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 長毛象、劍齒虎、大地懶等巨型動物在人類擴散後相繼滅絕。
events.desc_en: Mammoths, saber-toothed cats, giant sloths went extinct as humans spread globally.
events.amazon_title: End of the Megafauna: The Fate of the World's Hugest, Fiercest, and Strangest Animals — Ross D.E. MacPhee

events.desc_zh: 人類開始馴化狗、羊、牛、豬。農業革命改變人與動物的關係。
events.desc_en: Humans domesticated dogs, sheep, cattle, pigs. Agriculture transformed human-animal relationships.
events.amazon_title: How to Tame a Fox (and Build a Dog): Visionary Scientists and a Siberian Tale of Jump-Started Evolution — Lee Alan Dugatkin & Lyudmila Trut

events.zh: 查德沙赫人・最早人族?
events.desc_zh: 2001 年在查德發現，距今約 700 萬年。可能是最早的人族成員，但仍有爭議。腦容量與黑猩猩相近，但枕骨大孔位置暗示可能直立行走。
events.desc_en: Discovered in Chad in 2001, ~7 million years old. Possibly the earliest hominin, though debated. Brain size similar to chimpanzees, but foramen magnum position suggests possible bipedalism.
events.amazon_title: Fossil Men: The Quest for the Oldest Skeleton and the Origins of Humankind — Kermit Pattison

events.desc_zh: 2000 年在肯亞發現，距今約 600 萬年。股骨形態顯示可能已雙足行走，但仍生活在樹上。
events.desc_en: Discovered in Kenya in 2000, ~6 million years old. Femur morphology suggests possible bipedalism while still arboreal.
events.amazon_title: Life: An Unauthorised Biography — Richard Fortey

events.desc_zh: 1994 年在衣索比亞發現，距今約 440 萬年。能雙足行走也能攀樹。「Ardi」化石比「Lucy」更早 120 萬年，改寫了人類演化理解。
events.desc_en: Discovered in Ethiopia in 1994, ~4.4 million years old. Could walk bipedally and climb trees. 'Ardi' predates 'Lucy' by 1.2 million years, reshaping our understanding of human evolution.
events.amazon_title: Fossil Men: The Quest for the Oldest Skeleton and the Origins of Humankind — Kermit Pattison

events.desc_zh: 距今約 280-150 萬年。「能幹的人」，最早被歸入人屬（Homo）的物種。腦容量約 600cc，是第一個製造石器的人族。
events.desc_en: ~2.8-1.5 million years ago. 'Handy man', the first species classified in genus Homo. Brain ~600cc, first toolmaker.
events.amazon_title: Life: A Natural History of the First Four Billion Years of Life on Earth — Richard Fortey

events.en: Zhou Yi (Book of Changes Core Text)
events.desc_zh: 西周初年成書，以六十四卦及卦爻辭為主體，原為占卜用書，後成為中國最古老的哲學經典之一。
events.desc_en: Compiled in the early Western Zhou, consisting of 64 hexagrams and their oracle texts. Originally a divination manual, it became one of China's oldest philosophical classics.
events.amazon_title: The I Ching or Book of Changes — Richard Wilhelm

events.desc_zh: 戰國末期至漢初儒家對《周易》的哲學解釋合集，包含〈彖〉〈象〉〈繫辭〉等七種十篇，使易經從占卜書升格為哲學經典。傳統歸於孔子，現代學界認為是戰國諸儒所作。
events.desc_en: A Confucian philosophical commentary on the Zhou Yi compiled in the late Warring States to early Han period. The Ten Wings transformed the I Ching from a divination manual into a major philosophical classic. Traditionally attributed to Confucius, but modern scholars consider it the work of Warring States Confucian scholars.
events.amazon_title: I Ching: The Essential Translation — John Minford

events.desc_zh: 共和黨候選人林肯當選總統，反對奴隸制擴張，直接觸發南方各州脫離聯邦。
events.desc_en: Republican candidate Abraham Lincoln won the presidency on an anti-slavery-expansion platform, directly triggering Southern secession.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.zh: 邦聯成立·南方七州脫離
events.desc_zh: 南卡羅來納等七個南方州脫離聯邦，成立美利堅邦聯（CSA），傑佛遜·戴維斯任臨時總統。
events.desc_en: Seven Southern states seceded from the Union and formed the Confederate States of America, with Jefferson Davis as provisional president.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.zh: 桑姆特堡戰役·戰爭爆發
events.en: Battle of Fort Sumter — War Begins
events.desc_zh: 邦聯軍砲擊位於南卡羅來納州查爾斯頓港的桑姆特堡，南北戰爭正式開打。
events.desc_en: Confederate forces bombarded Fort Sumter in Charleston Harbor, South Carolina, formally beginning the American Civil War.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.desc_zh: 美國史上最血腥的單日戰役，傷亡 2.3 萬人。聯邦軍戰術勝利，使林肯得以發布解放奴隸宣言。
events.desc_en: The bloodiest single day in American history with 23,000 casualties. The Union's tactical victory enabled Lincoln to issue the Emancipation Proclamation.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.desc_zh: 林肯宣布所有邦聯轄區內的奴隸獲得自由，將內戰提升為解放戰爭，並阻止英法承認邦聯。
events.desc_en: Lincoln declared all enslaved people in Confederate territory free, transforming the war into a struggle for emancipation and preventing British and French recognition of the Confederacy.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.desc_zh: 聯邦軍擊敗李將軍領導的南軍，是南北戰爭轉捩點。三日交戰共 5 萬傷亡。
events.desc_en: Union forces defeated General Lee's Army of Northern Virginia, marking the war's turning point. Three days of fighting produced 50,000 casualties.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.desc_zh: 林肯在蓋茨堡國家公墓的兩分鐘演說，重新定義美國價值：「民有、民治、民享之政府」。
events.desc_en: Lincoln's two-minute speech at Gettysburg National Cemetery redefined American values: 'government of the people, by the people, for the people.'
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.zh: 阿波馬托克斯投降·戰爭結束
events.desc_zh: 李將軍在阿波馬托克斯郡府向格蘭特將軍投降，南北戰爭實質結束，南方失去主力。
events.desc_en: General Lee surrendered to General Grant at Appomattox Court House, effectively ending the Civil War with the South losing its main army.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.desc_zh: 戰爭結束 5 天後，林肯在福特劇院觀劇時遭演員布斯槍擊身亡，成為第一位遇刺的美國總統。
events.desc_en: Five days after the war ended, Lincoln was shot by actor John Wilkes Booth at Ford's Theatre, becoming the first U.S. president to be assassinated.
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.zh: 第十三條修正案·廢除奴隸制
events.desc_zh: 美國憲法第十三條修正案通過，正式廢除奴隸制度。南北戰爭最重要的法律成果。
events.desc_en: The 13th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution was ratified, formally abolishing slavery. The most important legal outcome of the Civil War.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/美國憲法第十三修正案
events.amazon_title: Battle Cry of Freedom: The Civil War Era — James M. McPherson

events.zh: 德國入侵波蘭·戰爭爆發
events.desc_zh: 1939 年 9 月 1 日，納粹德國閃電入侵波蘭，同月蘇聯依《德蘇互不侵犯條約》密約由東線進攻，英法對德宣戰，歐戰正式開打。
events.desc_en: On September 1, 1939, Nazi Germany launched a blitzkrieg on Poland; the Soviet Union invaded from the east per the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact. Britain and France declared war on Germany, beginning WWII in Europe.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 德軍快速推進下，盟軍 33.8 萬人自敦克爾克海灘撤往英國，動員大量民用船隻參與救援，史稱「敦克爾克奇蹟」。
events.desc_en: As German forces advanced rapidly, 338,000 Allied troops were evacuated from the beaches of Dunkirk to Britain with the help of civilian vessels — known as the 'Miracle of Dunkirk.'
events.amazon_title: The Miracle of Dunkirk — Walter Lord

events.zh: 法國淪陷·維琪政府成立
events.desc_zh: 德軍繞過馬奇諾防線由阿登森林突破，六週內攻陷巴黎。法國投降並設維琪政府，戴高樂赴倫敦組「自由法國」。
events.desc_en: German forces bypassed the Maginot Line through the Ardennes and captured Paris within six weeks. France surrendered and the Vichy regime was established; de Gaulle led Free France from London.
events.amazon_title: To Lose a Battle: France 1940 — Alistair Horne

events.desc_zh: 史上首場完全以空戰決定勝負的戰役。英國皇家空軍擊退德國空軍入侵準備，希特勒被迫放棄「海獅計畫」登陸英國。
events.desc_en: The first campaign decided entirely in the air. The RAF repelled the Luftwaffe, forcing Hitler to abandon Operation Sea Lion — the planned invasion of Britain.
events.amazon_title: The Battle of Britain: Five Months That Changed History — James Holland

events.zh: 巴巴羅薩行動·德軍侵蘇
events.desc_zh: 德國撕毀互不侵犯條約，集結 300 多萬兵力進攻蘇聯，開闢東線戰場，是人類史上規模最大的陸戰。
events.desc_en: Germany broke the non-aggression pact and invaded the Soviet Union with over 3 million troops, opening the Eastern Front — the largest land invasion in history.
events.amazon_title: When Titans Clashed: How the Red Army Stopped Hitler — David M. Glantz & Jonathan M. House

events.desc_zh: 1942 年 8 月至 1943 年 2 月，蘇軍在史達林格勒圍殲德國第六集團軍，是東線轉捩點，德國自此節節敗退。
events.desc_en: From August 1942 to February 1943, Soviet forces encircled and destroyed the German 6th Army at Stalingrad — the turning point of the Eastern Front.
events.amazon_title: Stalingrad: The Fateful Siege, 1942-1943 — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 史上最大規模坦克戰。蘇軍預判並阻止德軍夏季攻勢，德國裝甲部隊元氣大傷，東線主動權徹底易手。
events.desc_en: The largest tank battle in history. Soviet forces anticipated and halted Germany's summer offensive; German armored forces never recovered and the strategic initiative passed to the USSR.
events.amazon_title: Kursk: The Greatest Battle — Lloyd Clark

events.desc_zh: 1944 年 6 月 6 日，西方盟軍 15.6 萬人跨海在諾曼第搶灘，是史上最大規模兩棲作戰，開闢西線並加速德國崩潰。
events.desc_en: On June 6, 1944, 156,000 Western Allied troops landed on the beaches of Normandy — the largest amphibious assault in history — opening the Western Front.
events.amazon_title: D-Day: The Battle for Normandy — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 8 月 25 日，法國抵抗運動配合盟軍解放巴黎，戴高樂在香榭麗舍大道遊行，維琪政權瓦解。
events.desc_en: On August 25, French Resistance forces, aided by the Allies, liberated Paris. De Gaulle marched down the Champs-Élysées as the Vichy regime collapsed.
events.amazon_title: Is Paris Burning? — Larry Collins & Dominique Lapierre

events.zh: 阿登反擊戰·突出部之役
events.desc_zh: 德軍最後一次大規模反攻，試圖奪回安特衛普分割盟軍。激戰一個多月，德軍耗盡戰略預備隊後徹底失守。
events.desc_en: Germany's final major counteroffensive, aimed at recapturing Antwerp and splitting the Allied armies. After over a month of fighting, Germany exhausted its strategic reserves.
events.amazon_title: A Time for Trumpets: The Untold Story of the Battle of the Bulge — Charles B. MacDonald

events.desc_zh: 蘇軍 250 萬兵力攻入柏林，4 月 30 日希特勒在總理府地堡自殺，蘇聯紅旗在國會大廈升起。
events.desc_en: Soviet forces with 2.5 million troops stormed Berlin. On April 30, Hitler committed suicide in the Führerbunker; the Soviet flag was raised over the Reichstag.
events.amazon_title: The Fall of Berlin 1945 — Antony Beevor

events.zh: 德國無條件投降·歐戰結束
events.desc_zh: 1945 年 5 月 8 日，德軍在蘭斯與柏林分別簽署投降書，歐洲戰場正式結束，是為 V-E Day。
events.desc_en: On May 8, 1945, German forces signed instruments of surrender in Reims and Berlin, formally ending the war in Europe — V-E Day.
events.amazon_title: The Fall of Berlin 1945 — Antony Beevor

events.zh: 盧溝橋事變·中日全面開戰
events.desc_zh: 7 月 7 日日軍藉演習失蹤為由砲擊宛平城，中日全面戰爭爆發，亞洲戰場遠早於歐洲開打。
events.desc_en: On July 7, Japanese forces shelled Wanping using a missing-soldier pretext, triggering all-out war between China and Japan — beginning the Asian theatre of WWII years before Europe.
events.amazon_title: Forgotten Ally: China's World War II, 1937-1945 — Rana Mitter

events.desc_zh: 12 月日軍攻陷南京後，於六週內屠殺約 30 萬平民與戰俘，為 20 世紀最嚴重戰爭暴行之一。
events.desc_en: After capturing Nanjing in December, Japanese forces massacred an estimated 300,000 civilians and POWs over six weeks — one of the worst atrocities of the 20th century.
events.amazon_title: The Rape of Nanking: The Forgotten Holocaust of World War II — Iris Chang

events.desc_zh: 12 月 7 日日軍偷襲夏威夷珍珠港美軍基地，擊沉多艘戰艦，隔日美國對日宣戰，正式加入二戰。
events.desc_en: On December 7, Japanese forces surprise-attacked the U.S. Pacific Fleet at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, sinking several battleships. The U.S. declared war the next day, formally entering WWII.
events.amazon_title: At Dawn We Slept: The Untold Story of Pearl Harbor — Gordon W. Prange

events.desc_zh: 英軍 8 萬人向日軍投降，邱吉爾稱為「英國軍史上最嚴重的災難」。新加坡被日軍占領三年半。
events.desc_en: 80,000 British Commonwealth troops surrendered to Japanese forces. Churchill called it 'the worst disaster in British military history.' Singapore was occupied by Japan for three and a half years.
events.amazon_title: Singapore, 1942: Britain's Greatest Defeat — Alan Warren

events.desc_zh: 史上首場航母對決，敵艦未曾互見。美國阻止日軍進攻莫爾斯比港，是日本在太平洋首次戰略失利。
events.desc_en: The first naval battle fought entirely between aircraft carriers, with opposing fleets never sighting each other. The U.S. thwarted Japan's advance on Port Moresby — Japan's first strategic setback.
events.amazon_title: Shattered Sword: The Untold Story of the Battle of Midway — Jonathan Parshall & Anthony Tully

events.zh: 中途島海戰·太平洋轉捩點
events.desc_zh: 6 月美軍因破譯日軍密碼伏擊並擊沉日本四艘主力航母，日本海軍元氣大傷，太平洋戰場主動權易手。
events.desc_en: In June, U.S. forces, having broken Japanese codes, ambushed and sank four Japanese fleet carriers. Japan's naval supremacy collapsed and strategic initiative shifted to the Allies.
events.amazon_title: Shattered Sword: The Untold Story of the Battle of Midway — Jonathan Parshall & Anthony Tully

events.desc_zh: 盟軍首次大規模登陸反攻，歷時半年拿下瓜達康納爾島，打破日軍南太平洋攻勢，「跳島戰術」由此展開。
events.desc_en: The first major Allied offensive amphibious operation. Six months of fighting secured Guadalcanal, broke Japan's South Pacific advance, and began the island-hopping campaign.
events.amazon_title: Neptune's Inferno: The U.S. Navy at Guadalcanal — James D. Hornfischer

events.desc_zh: 史上最大規模海戰。日本海軍主力遭摧毀並首次動用神風特攻隊，美軍收復菲律賓的重要一役。
events.desc_en: The largest naval battle in history. Japan's main fleet was destroyed and kamikaze tactics first appeared. A decisive step in the U.S. recapture of the Philippines.
events.amazon_title: The Last Stand of the Tin Can Sailors: The Extraordinary World War II Story of the U.S. Navy's Finest Hour — James D. Hornfischer

events.desc_zh: 5 週血戰，美軍以近 2.7 萬傷亡攻下該島。美聯社記者拍下星條旗插上摺缽山的經典照片。
events.desc_en: Five weeks of brutal combat cost U.S. forces nearly 27,000 casualties. Joe Rosenthal's iconic photo of the flag-raising on Mount Suribachi became a symbol of the Pacific War.
events.amazon_title: Flags of Our Fathers — James Bradley

events.desc_zh: 太平洋最血腥陸戰，雙方死傷逾 20 萬，含大量沖繩平民。該役慘烈促使美方最終選擇原子彈而非本土登陸。
events.desc_en: The bloodiest battle of the Pacific War with over 200,000 casualties, including many Okinawan civilians. Its carnage influenced the U.S. choice of atomic bombs over invading the Japanese home islands.
events.amazon_title: With the Old Breed: At Peleliu and Okinawa — E.B. Sledge

events.en: Atomic Bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
events.desc_zh: 8 月 6 日廣島、9 日長崎先後遭美軍投下原子彈，造成 20 多萬人死亡，是人類首次也是唯一一次戰爭中動用核武。
events.desc_en: On August 6 and 9, U.S. forces dropped atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki, killing over 200,000 people. The first and only wartime use of nuclear weapons.
events.amazon_title: Hiroshima — John Hersey

events.zh: 日本投降·密蘇里號受降
events.desc_zh: 8 月 15 日昭和天皇玉音放送宣布接受波茨坦宣言，9 月 2 日在東京灣密蘇里號戰艦簽署投降書，二戰正式結束。
events.desc_en: On August 15, Emperor Hirohito announced acceptance of the Potsdam Declaration. On September 2, Japan signed the instrument of surrender aboard USS Missouri in Tokyo Bay, ending WWII.
events.amazon_title: Downfall: The End of the Imperial Japanese Empire — Richard B. Frank

events.desc_zh: 1 月 30 日希特勒被任命為德國總理，隨後藉國會縱火案通過《授權法》，納粹黨取得獨裁權力，威瑪共和終結。
events.desc_en: On January 30, Hitler was appointed Chancellor of Germany. Using the Reichstag fire as pretext, the Nazis passed the Enabling Act, seizing dictatorial power and ending the Weimar Republic.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 納粹德國頒布反猶太法律，剝奪猶太人公民權、禁止異族通婚，系統性迫害自此法制化。
events.desc_en: Nazi Germany enacted antisemitic laws stripping Jews of citizenship and banning intermarriage, institutionalizing systematic persecution.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 納粹德國以柏林奧運粉飾形象，同年與義大利簽「柏林-羅馬軸心」、與日本簽《反共產國際協定》，軸心雛形成立。
events.desc_en: Nazi Germany used the Berlin Olympics as propaganda. In the same year, Germany signed the Rome–Berlin Axis and the Anti-Comintern Pact with Japan — the Axis alliance was forming.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.zh: 慕尼黑協定·綏靖政策高峰
events.desc_zh: 英法同意德國併吞捷克蘇台德地區，張伯倫宣稱「這一代的和平」。綏靖政策成為縱容侵略的代名詞。
events.desc_en: Britain and France allowed Germany to annex Czechoslovakia's Sudetenland. Chamberlain proclaimed 'peace for our time.' Appeasement became synonymous with enabling aggression.
events.amazon_title: Munich, 1938: Appeasement and World War II — David Faber

events.desc_zh: 11 月 9 日納粹組織全德反猶暴動，摧毀猶太教堂與商店達 7 千間，是大屠殺升級的分水嶺。
events.desc_en: On November 9, Nazis organized nationwide pogroms across Germany, destroying ~7,000 Jewish synagogues and shops. A turning point toward the Holocaust's escalation.
events.amazon_title: Kristallnacht: Prelude to Destruction — Martin Gilbert

events.desc_zh: 愛因斯坦與西拉德聯名致信羅斯福，警告德國可能研發原子彈，促使美國啟動曼哈頓計畫。
events.desc_en: Einstein and Szilard jointly wrote to Roosevelt warning that Germany might develop an atomic bomb, spurring the United States to launch the Manhattan Project.
events.amazon_title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb: 25th Anniversary Edition — Richard Rhodes

events.desc_zh: 3 月美國國會通過租借法案，向英蘇等盟國提供總值 500 億美元的軍火物資，實質打破中立走向參戰。
events.desc_en: In March, the U.S. Congress passed the Lend-Lease Act, supplying Allies with $50 billion in military aid — effectively ending American neutrality.
events.amazon_title: The Second World War — Antony Beevor

events.zh: 萬湖會議·最終解決方案
events.desc_zh: 1 月 20 日納粹高層在柏林萬湖別墅會議上正式協調「猶太人問題的最終解決方案」，系統性屠殺全面啟動。
events.desc_en: On January 20, senior Nazi officials met at a Wannsee villa to coordinate the 'Final Solution to the Jewish Question,' launching the systematic genocide.
events.amazon_title: The Wannsee Conference and the Final Solution: A Reconsideration — Mark Roseman

events.zh: 大屠殺·奧斯威辛集中營
events.desc_zh: 1942-1945 年納粹系統性屠殺約 600 萬猶太人，以奧斯威辛-比克瑙滅絕營為最大執行中心，另有數百萬羅姆人、蘇聯戰俘、同性戀者等受害。
events.desc_en: From 1942–1945, Nazis systematically murdered about 6 million Jews, with Auschwitz-Birkenau as the largest killing center. Millions more Romani, Soviet POWs, and others were also victims.
events.amazon_title: The Holocaust: A New History — Laurence Rees

events.desc_zh: 11 月羅斯福、邱吉爾、史達林三巨頭首次會面，確定開闢西線、戰後歐洲格局與蘇聯對日作戰承諾。
events.desc_en: In November, Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin met for the first time, agreeing on opening the Western Front, postwar European borders, and Soviet entry into the Pacific war.
events.amazon_title: The Second World War — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 2 月雅爾達決定戰後歐洲勢力範圍與蘇聯出兵對日；7 月波茨坦會議對日發布最後通牒並劃定德國占領區，冷戰雛形從此浮現。
events.desc_en: In February, Yalta divided postwar Europe and secured Soviet entry against Japan; in July, Potsdam issued the ultimatum to Japan and set up occupation zones — the Cold War's early contours.
events.amazon_title: The Second World War — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 10 月 24 日《聯合國憲章》生效，取代失敗的國際聯盟，設安全理事會五常任理事國，為戰後國際秩序定基。
events.desc_en: On October 24, the UN Charter came into force, replacing the failed League of Nations and establishing the Security Council's five permanent members — the foundation of the postwar order.
events.amazon_title: The Second World War — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 1945-1946 年起盟軍分別在紐倫堡與東京審判納粹德國與日本的戰犯，首次確立「反人類罪」與「侵略罪」國際法概念。
events.desc_en: Starting 1945–1946, the Allies tried Nazi German and Japanese war criminals in Nuremberg and Tokyo, establishing crimes against humanity and aggression as international law.
events.amazon_title: Nuremberg: Infamy on Trial — Joseph E. Persico

events.desc_zh: 墨索里尼為建構「新羅馬帝國」入侵衣索比亞，動用毒氣；國聯制裁失敗，標誌集體安全體系崩潰，加速二戰醞釀。
events.desc_en: Mussolini invaded Ethiopia to revive a 'New Roman Empire,' using chemical weapons. The League of Nations' failed sanctions marked the collapse of collective security and hastened WWII.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/第二次義大利衣索比亞戰爭
events.amazon_title: Mussolini's War: Fascist Italy from Triumph to Collapse, 1935-1943 — John Gooch

events.zh: 義大利參戰·地中海戰線開啟
events.desc_zh: 6 月法國敗局已定時，墨索里尼對英法宣戰，義大利攻擊法國南部與北非、巴爾幹，地中海與北非成為英義長期交戰前線。
events.desc_en: In June, as France was collapsing, Mussolini declared war on Britain and France. Italy attacked southern France, North Africa, and the Balkans, turning the Mediterranean into a long British–Italian front.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/義大利王國參與第二次世界大戰
events.amazon_title: Mussolini's War: Fascist Italy from Triumph to Collapse, 1935-1943 — John Gooch

events.zh: 巴爾幹戰役·希臘與克里特
events.desc_zh: 義軍入侵希臘受挫，德國出兵迅速拿下南斯拉夫與希臘。5 月德軍空降攻陷克里特島。此役消耗德軍資源並延後巴巴羅薩行動。
events.desc_en: After Italy's invasion of Greece stalled, Germany swept through Yugoslavia and Greece. In May, German paratroopers captured Crete. The campaign drained German resources and delayed Operation Barbarossa.
events.amazon_title: The Second World War — Antony Beevor

events.desc_zh: 10-11 月蒙哥馬利率英軍第八軍團擊敗隆美爾的德義非洲軍團，守住埃及與蘇伊士運河。邱吉爾稱「敗仗之前我們從未戰勝，此後也從未戰敗」。
events.desc_en: In October–November, Montgomery's Eighth Army defeated Rommel's German–Italian Afrika Korps, saving Egypt and the Suez. Churchill said: 'Before Alamein we never had a victory. After Alamein we never had a defeat.'
events.amazon_title: Alamein — Stephen Bungay

events.zh: 火炬行動·盟軍登陸北非
events.desc_zh: 11 月美英聯合於摩洛哥、阿爾及利亞登陸，艾森豪首次擔任大型登陸作戰總指揮。與阿拉曼前後夾擊，半年後北非軸心軍投降。
events.desc_en: In November, U.S. and British forces jointly landed in Morocco and Algeria, Eisenhower's first major amphibious command. Combined with El Alamein, Axis forces in North Africa surrendered within six months.
events.amazon_title: An Army at Dawn: The War in North Africa, 1942-1943 — Rick Atkinson

events.zh: 墨索里尼倒台·義大利投降
events.desc_zh: 7 月盟軍登陸西西里，義大利法西斯大議會罷黜墨索里尼。9 月新政府對盟軍投降，德軍隨即占領北義並救出墨索里尼扶植薩羅共和國。
events.desc_en: In July, Allied forces landed in Sicily and the Fascist Grand Council deposed Mussolini. In September, the new government surrendered; Germany occupied northern Italy and installed a rump Salò Republic.
events.amazon_title: Mussolini's War: Fascist Italy from Triumph to Collapse, 1935-1943 — John Gooch

events.desc_zh: 1 月盟軍於安齊奧登陸繞過古斯塔夫防線，經蒙特卡西諾激戰，6 月 4 日解放羅馬，是軸心國首都首次陷落，兩日後即遭 D-Day 新聞蓋過。
events.desc_en: Allied forces landed at Anzio in January to outflank the Gustav Line, and after fierce fighting at Monte Cassino, liberated Rome on June 4 — the first Axis capital to fall, overshadowed by D-Day two days later.
events.amazon_title: The Day of Battle: The War in Sicily and Italy, 1943-1944 — Rick Atkinson

events.zh: V-1 / V-2 飛彈轟炸倫敦
events.desc_zh: 6 月起德國以 V-1 巡弋飛彈、9 月起以 V-2 彈道飛彈攻擊倫敦，是史上首度使用巡弋與彈道飛彈的戰爭，也奠定戰後火箭與太空科技基礎。
events.desc_en: From June Germany launched V-1 cruise missiles and from September V-2 ballistic missiles at London — the first combat use of cruise and ballistic missiles, laying the groundwork for postwar rocketry and spaceflight.
events.amazon_title: Europe: A History — Norman Davies

events.zh: 馬來亞戰役·日軍入侵東南亞
events.desc_zh: 與珍珠港同日展開。日軍山下奉文 70 天內推進 1,100 公里，以少勝多擊敗英軍，威脅東南亞殖民體系。
events.desc_en: Launched the same day as Pearl Harbor. In 70 days, General Yamashita advanced 1,100 km, defeating larger British forces and shaking the Southeast Asian colonial order.
events.amazon_title: Singapore Burning: Heroism and Surrender in World War II — Colin Smith

events.zh: 菲律賓陷落·巴丹死亡行軍
events.en: Fall of the Philippines & Bataan Death March
events.desc_zh: 4 月美菲聯軍在巴丹半島投降，日軍強迫 7.6 萬戰俘徒步百公里，沿途虐殺近 2 萬人；麥克阿瑟留下「I shall return」名言撤往澳洲。
events.desc_en: In April, U.S.–Filipino forces surrendered at Bataan. Japan forced 76,000 POWs to march ~100 km, killing nearly 20,000 along the way. MacArthur famously pledged 'I shall return' on his withdrawal to Australia.
events.amazon_title: Tears in the Darkness: The Story of the Bataan Death March and Its Aftermath — Michael & Elizabeth Norman

events.desc_zh: 4 月 18 日美軍 16 架 B-25 自大黃蜂號航母起飛，首次轟炸東京等日本本土都市，實質損害有限但重挫日本民心，並促成日方冒險進攻中途島。
events.desc_en: On April 18, 16 U.S. B-25s launched from USS Hornet — the first bombing of the Japanese home islands. Material damage was light but morale impact was large, and it drove Japan to the Midway gamble.
events.amazon_title: Target Tokyo: Jimmy Doolittle and the Raid That Avenged Pearl Harbor — James M. Scott

events.desc_zh: 1942-1945 年橫跨三年半的熱帶叢林戰。日軍切斷滇緬公路後，英軍、中國遠征軍（史迪威、孫立人）及印度軍聯合反攻，1945 年光復仰光。
events.desc_en: A 3.5-year jungle war (1942–1945). After Japan severed the Burma Road, British forces, the Chinese Expeditionary Force (Stilwell, Sun Li-jen), and Indian troops jointly counterattacked, liberating Rangoon in 1945.
events.amazon_title: Defeat Into Victory: Battling Japan in Burma and India, 1942-1945 — William Slim

events.desc_zh: 6-7 月美軍攻下塞班島，突破日本「絕對國防圈」，從此 B-29 可直達東京。東條英機內閣因此下台。
events.desc_en: From June–July, U.S. forces captured Saipan, breaching Japan's 'absolute defensive line.' B-29s could now reach Tokyo directly. The Tōjō cabinet fell as a result.
events.amazon_title: Downfall: The End of the Imperial Japanese Empire — Richard B. Frank

events.zh: 馬里亞納海戰·火雞大獵殺
events.desc_zh: 6 月 19-20 日，美軍一日擊落 400 餘架日機、擊沉 3 艘航母，美方飛行員戲稱「馬里亞納火雞大獵殺」。日本海軍航空兵自此徹底瓦解。
events.desc_en: On June 19–20, U.S. forces shot down over 400 Japanese aircraft and sank 3 carriers in a day — U.S. pilots dubbed it 'the Great Marianas Turkey Shoot.' Japan's naval aviation was annihilated.
events.amazon_title: Shattered Sword: The Untold Story of the Battle of Midway — Jonathan Parshall & Anthony Tully

events.zh: 東京大空襲·燃燒彈攻擊
events.desc_zh: 3 月 10 日美軍 B-29 以低空燃燒彈攻擊東京，木造民居大片燒毀，單日死亡逾 10 萬人，為二戰最致命的單日常規轟炸。
events.desc_en: On March 10, U.S. B-29s dropped incendiary bombs on Tokyo at low altitude, burning wooden districts to the ground and killing over 100,000 in a single day — the deadliest conventional air raid of WWII.
events.amazon_title: Black Snow: Curtis LeMay, the Firebombing of Tokyo, and the Road to the Atomic Bomb — James M. Scott

events.zh: 蘇聯出兵滿洲·關東軍覆滅
events.desc_zh: 8 月 9 日蘇軍依雅爾達密約對日宣戰，150 萬大軍橫掃滿洲，11 天內擊潰關東軍，與原子彈雙重衝擊促成日本決定投降。
events.desc_en: On August 9, the USSR declared war per the Yalta accords. 1.5 million Soviet troops overran Manchuria and crushed the Kwantung Army in 11 days. Combined with the atomic bombs, this drove Japan's decision to surrender.
events.amazon_title: Downfall: The End of the Imperial Japanese Empire — Richard B. Frank

events.en: Germany Leaves the League of Nations
events.desc_zh: 10 月希特勒宣布德國退出國際聯盟與裁軍會議，擺脫凡爾賽條約束縛，是納粹德國重整軍備的先聲。
events.desc_en: In October, Hitler withdrew Germany from the League of Nations and the Disarmament Conference, escaping the Versailles framework — the opening move toward Nazi rearmament.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.zh: 德國重整軍備·恢復徵兵
events.desc_zh: 3 月希特勒公開毀棄凡爾賽條約軍事條款、恢復徵兵並成立空軍，英法抗議但未實質制裁，綏靖態勢確立。
events.desc_en: In March, Hitler openly repudiated the Versailles military clauses, reinstated conscription, and unveiled the Luftwaffe. Britain and France protested but took no action — appeasement took hold.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 3 月德軍進駐非軍事區萊茵蘭，直接違反凡爾賽與羅加諾條約。法國未反應、英國綏靖，希特勒擴張信心大增。
events.desc_en: In March, German troops entered the demilitarized Rhineland, directly violating Versailles and Locarno. France did not respond and Britain appeased — boosting Hitler's confidence in further expansion.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 1936-1939 年西班牙內戰中，德義派兵助佛朗哥（禿鷹軍團、格爾尼卡轟炸），蘇聯援共和軍，成為二戰前的實戰演習場。
events.desc_en: During the 1936–1939 Spanish Civil War, Germany and Italy backed Franco (Condor Legion, Guernica bombing), while the USSR supported the Republicans — a rehearsal for WWII.
events.amazon_title: The Spanish Civil War: Revised Edition — Hugh Thomas

events.desc_zh: 3 月德軍進入奧地利，奧國被納入大德意志帝國，公投以 99% 支持合法化。英法再次未抵抗，希特勒擴張如入無人之境。
events.desc_en: In March, German troops entered Austria and it was absorbed into the Greater German Reich, legitimized by a 99% plebiscite. Britain and France again stood aside as Hitler expanded unopposed.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.desc_zh: 3 月德軍進入布拉格，併吞整個捷克，徹底撕毀慕尼黑協定。英法終於意識綏靖失敗，開始對波蘭等提供軍事保證。
events.desc_en: In March, German troops entered Prague and annexed the rest of Czechoslovakia, shredding the Munich Agreement. Britain and France finally recognized the failure of appeasement and issued guarantees to Poland.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.zh: 義大利入侵阿爾巴尼亞
events.desc_zh: 4 月墨索里尼出兵一週內占領阿爾巴尼亞，建立傀儡王國並納入義大利帝國版圖，為南歐擴張的前站。
events.desc_en: In April, Mussolini invaded and occupied Albania within a week, installing a puppet kingdom and absorbing it into the Italian Empire — a staging ground for southern European expansion.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/義大利入侵阿爾巴尼亞
events.amazon_title: Mussolini's War: Fascist Italy from Triumph to Collapse, 1935-1943 — John Gooch

events.zh: 德蘇互不侵犯條約·莫洛托夫
events.desc_zh: 8 月 23 日德蘇簽約含密約劃分東歐勢力範圍，使希特勒解除雙線作戰顧慮，9 天後入侵波蘭。後來被巴巴羅薩撕毀。
events.desc_en: Signed August 23, the Nazi–Soviet pact included a secret protocol partitioning Eastern Europe, freeing Hitler from two-front worries. Nine days later, Germany invaded Poland. The pact was later broken by Barbarossa.
events.amazon_title: The Devils' Alliance: Hitler's Pact with Stalin, 1939-1941 — Roger Moorhouse

events.zh: 大西洋戰役·U 艇群狼戰術
events.desc_zh: 1939-1945 年貫穿全戰爭最長戰役。德軍 U 艇群狼戰術攻擊盟軍運補船團，1943 年盟軍憑密碼破譯（Ultra）與護航航母逆轉。
events.desc_en: The longest campaign of the war (1939–1945). German U-boats hunted Allied convoys in 'wolfpack' tactics until 1943, when Ultra code-breaking and escort carriers turned the tide.
events.amazon_title: The Battle of the Atlantic: How the Allies Won the War — Jonathan Dimbleby

events.desc_zh: 8 月波蘭地下國民軍趁蘇軍逼近華沙時起義反德，蘇軍在河對岸按兵不動。63 天激戰後起義失敗，華沙遭德軍夷平。
events.desc_en: In August, the Polish Home Army rose against Germans as Soviet forces approached Warsaw. The Red Army halted across the Vistula. After 63 days of fighting, the uprising was crushed and Warsaw was razed.
events.amazon_title: Rising '44: The Battle for Warsaw — Norman Davies

events.desc_zh: 9 月 18 日日本關東軍自導自演柳條湖鐵路爆炸，藉機侵占中國東北三省。國聯派李頓調查團譴責日本，日本因此退出國聯。
events.desc_en: On September 18, the Japanese Kwantung Army staged the Liutiaohu railway bombing as a pretext to invade Manchuria. The Lytton Commission condemned Japan, leading it to quit the League of Nations.
events.amazon_title: Japan's Total Empire: Manchuria and the Culture of Wartime Imperialism — Louise Young

events.desc_zh: 3 月日本扶植清朝末代皇帝溥儀在長春建立傀儡國「滿洲國」，掌控中國東北資源與工業，為侵華跳板。
events.desc_en: In March, Japan installed the last Qing emperor Puyi as head of the puppet state Manchukuo in Changchun, controlling northeast China's resources and industry — a springboard for further invasion.
events.amazon_title: Japan's Total Empire: Manchuria and the Culture of Wartime Imperialism — Louise Young

events.zh: 武漢會戰·國民政府遷重慶
events.desc_zh: 1938 年 6-10 月中日軍隊在武漢周邊激戰，日軍以重大代價攻占武漢。國民政府西遷重慶抗戰，抗戰進入相持階段。
events.desc_en: From June to October 1938, Chinese and Japanese forces fought around Wuhan. Japan took the city at heavy cost; the Nationalist government relocated to Chongqing, and the war entered a stalemate phase.
events.amazon_title: Forgotten Ally: China's World War II, 1937-1945 — Rana Mitter

events.zh: 三國同盟條約·日進駐法屬印支
events.desc_zh: 9 月 27 日德義日三國在柏林簽訂同盟條約，正式結為軸心國。同年日軍進駐法屬印度支那北部，美國對日禁運加碼。
events.desc_en: On September 27, Germany, Italy, and Japan signed the Tripartite Pact in Berlin, formally forming the Axis. That year Japan occupied northern French Indochina, prompting tighter U.S. embargoes.
events.amazon_title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich — William L. Shirer

events.en: Formosa Air Battle / Taipei Air Raid
events.desc_zh: 10 月起美軍轟炸日本殖民下台灣，摧毀高雄、新竹、台北等軍事與民生設施。1945 年 5 月 31 日台北大空襲造成三千餘人死亡。
events.desc_en: From October, U.S. forces bombed Japanese-colonized Taiwan, destroying military and civilian facilities in Kaohsiung, Hsinchu, and Taipei. The Taipei raid of May 31, 1945 killed over 3,000.

events.desc_zh: 4 月 18 日美軍破譯密碼後派 P-38 在布干維爾島上空擊落山本五十六座機，襲擊珍珠港的策劃者身亡，重挫日本海軍士氣。
events.desc_en: On April 18, U.S. P-38s, guided by broken Japanese codes, shot down Admiral Yamamoto's plane over Bougainville. The architect of the Pearl Harbor attack was dead, crushing Japanese navy morale.
events.amazon_title: Shattered Sword: The Untold Story of the Battle of Midway — Jonathan Parshall & Anthony Tully

events.desc_zh: 11 月 20-23 日美軍攻擊吉爾伯特群島塔拉瓦環礁，為中太平洋跳島戰術的首役。76 小時血戰中美軍傷亡慘重，促成兩棲作戰改革。
events.desc_en: On November 20–23, U.S. forces assaulted Tarawa Atoll in the Gilberts — the opening battle of the central Pacific island-hopping campaign. The costly 76-hour fight reshaped amphibious doctrine.

events.zh: 第一次伊普爾·塹壕戰定型
